theme: weaving a fast gossip network across a long ridge of villages
title: Weaving a Fast Gossip Network Across a Long Ridge of Villages
seed_problem: n points with fixed positions on a line, degree cap d, wirelength budget; maximize the algebraic connectivity (spectral gap) of the chosen graph
mutation: composite: villages are placed via a recursive fold hierarchy (every level contributes the same total fold-length, so wirelength is geometrically summable across scales) instead of arbitrary/uniform points; the objective couples that hierarchical-geometric layout to a wirelength-budget constraint AND a spectral-gap objective in one scorer, so the winning topology must exploit the fold structure directly rather than just avoiding long edges or building a generic expander
strategies: trivial: connect the plain path along the ridge (village i -- i+1); reproduces the checker's own baseline exactly
naive greedy: weave a local mesh, connecting nearest villages first (bandwidth 1,2,3,...) until degree cap/budget is exhausted -- never crosses a big fold, stays locally dense but globally thin

insight (strong): recursively bisect the village index range and add two fold-crossing bridge edges per split (extremes of each half-pair) at every level -- an index-only, coordinate-free hierarchical topology with O(log n) max degree and O(n) edges whose wirelength stays low because the fold-gaps are geometrically summable across levels, giving O(log n) hop-diameter (near-expander spectral gap) for a fraction of a naive strategy's wirelength
